issues.title: Mandatory Terraform state backend costs are missing from line items
issues.description: The architecture explicitly requires remote Terraform state in Azure Blob Storage, but the cost estimate does not include a state storage account/container, transactions, or retention/versioning overhead.
issues.recommendation: Add an explicit line item for Terraform state backend (storage + transactions + optional versioning/soft-delete) with low/expected/high monthly ranges.

issues.title: Unresolved MCP pricing is used to support a narrow total range
issues.description: Only App Service B1 is MCP-confirmed, while SQL, Application Insights, and Log Analytics are unresolved. Despite this, the artifact presents a tight total (~$25-$60) and a stable trend narrative, which overstates certainty.
issues.location: Cost At-a-Glance; Decision Summary; Detailed Cost Breakdown
issues.recommendation: Reframe totals into scenario bands (best/expected/worst) and widen uncertainty until unresolved SKUs are validated.

issues.title: Log Analytics treatment as 'bundled' can understate monitoring costs
issues.description: The table labels Log Analytics as '$0 bundled with AI', but workspace-based Application Insights and platform diagnostic logs are still meter-driven ingestion/retention costs.
issues.location: Detailed Cost Breakdown (Log Analytics row); Top 5 Cost Drivers
issues.recommendation: Clarify unified billing model: App Insights and workspace logs share ingestion meters; add separate assumptions for application telemetry vs platform diagnostic logs.

issues.title: Governance-driven cost uplift is not modeled
issues.description: The estimate assumes permissive dev networking/security. If subscription policies enforce private networking, diagnostics, or security controls, the estimate can jump materially.
issues.location: What We Are Not Paying For; Cost Risk Indicators; Decision Summary
issues.recommendation: Add a 'policy-constrained scenario' with incremental monthly uplift for likely enforced controls, pending Step 4 governance discovery.

issues.title: Savings recommendation includes weak-fit SQL Serverless option
issues.description: The artifact suggests SQL Serverless savings for this small always-on API profile, but serverless is not typically cost-optimal for continuously active workloads.
issues.recommendation: Replace with a conditional recommendation: evaluate only if usage is highly intermittent with measurable auto-pause windows.

issues.title: Decision matrix lacks cumulative dev-to-prod transition total
issues.description: The quick matrix lists individual add-ons but does not provide a consolidated minimum production monthly total.
issues.location: Quick Decision Matrix; What We Are Not Paying For
issues.recommendation: Add one row with cumulative 'minimum production-ready uplift' and resulting total monthly range.

issues.title: Cross-artifact cost narrative is inconsistent and unreconciled
issues.description: Requirements/architecture artifacts use higher working estimates (~$75-$150), while this document anchors on ~$25-$60 without reconciling the delta.
issues.location: Cost At-a-Glance; references to prior artifacts
issues.recommendation: Add a reconciliation subsection showing why earlier ranges changed and which estimate should be treated as authoritative.

issues.title: Risk indicators identify threats but not trigger thresholds
issues.description: Cost risks are listed, but there are no explicit cost or usage thresholds tied to specific actions.
issues.recommendation: Define quantitative triggers (e.g., daily ingestion >X GB for Y days) and corresponding mitigation actions with expected cost impact.

summary: The estimate is directionally plausible for a dev-only storefront and clearly under the stated budget envelope, but confidence is reduced by unresolved pricing inputs, missing mandatory state-backend costs, and ambiguous monitoring billing assumptions. The largest practical risk is underestimating total recurring spend once governance constraints and full telemetry ingestion are accounted for.
